meta.name: CD-RISC-2 Connor-Davidson Resilience Scale
meta.description: The CD-RISC-2 is a 2-item ultra-brief version of the Connor-Davidson Resilience Scale, extracting the two most core items from the original 25-item scale for rapid assessment of resilience to stress and adversity.
meta.author: Vaishnavi, Connor & Davidson (2007)
meta.reference: Vaishnavi S, Connor K, Davidson JRT. An abbreviated version of the Connor-Davidson Resilience Scale (CD-RISC), the CD-RISC2: psychometric properties and applications in psychopharmacological trials. Psychiatry Res. 2007;152(2-3):293-297.
meta.instruction: Please indicate how much the following statements have applied to you over the past month.

questions.text: Tend to bounce back after illness or hardship

ranges.label: Low Resilience
ranges.description: Your resilience level is low, suggesting significant difficulty adapting to change and recovering from adversity. You may feel overwhelmed by challenges that others seem to handle more easily.
suggestions: Seek social support from trusted friends, family, or community members rather than facing difficulties alone
Learn and practice specific techniques for adapting to change, such as problem-solving and cognitive reframing
Consider working with a mental health professional to develop and strengthen your resilience skills
Start with small, manageable challenges and gradually build your confidence in coping with adversity

ranges.label: Moderate Resilience
ranges.description: Your resilience is at a moderate level, indicating that you can cope with some stressors reasonably well but may struggle when facing more significant challenges or multiple pressures simultaneously.
suggestions: Continue developing your coping skills through deliberate practice and reflection on past successes
Maintain and strengthen your positive social network as it serves as a crucial buffer against stress
Build resilience through regular exercise, healthy sleep habits, and consistent self-care routines
Practice positive self-talk and cognitive reframing to shift your perspective on challenging situations
Set realistic goals and celebrate small achievements to build confidence in your ability to overcome obstacles

ranges.label: High Resilience
ranges.description: You demonstrate high resilience, indicating a strong ability to adapt to change, recover from setbacks, and maintain psychological well-being even during stressful periods. This is a valuable personal strength.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your strong adaptability and coping strategies that have served you well
Consider mentoring or supporting others who are struggling, as your resilience can inspire those around you
While helping others, remember to attend to your own self-care and recognize your own limits
